L1.area: System module data access and persistence mapping layer

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-controllable parameters are directly interpolated into SQL query strings using string substitution syntax instead of parameterized query bindings. An attacker can manipulate these parameters to inject arbitrary SQL, potentially allowing unauthorized data access, modification, or deletion.

L3.area: System user and dictionary data mapper definitions that perform dynamic field-based update and query operations
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where a field name parameter is directly interpolated into an SQL statement using string substitution rather than parameterized binding. When the application performs null-value cleanup operations on user records, an attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through the field name parameter, potentially allowing unauthorized data access, modification, or deletion.

ground_truth.reason: The updateNullByEmptyString MyBatis mapper query uses ${fieldName} string interpolation instead of parameterized #{} binding, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary SQL through the field name parameter.
